reasoning: Broad occupational categories like "Management, professional, and related occupations" and "Sales and office occupations" shift their proportion of the total workforce extremely slowly. The percentage was ~62% in 2022. For it to drop to ≤60.0% by Q4 2025, there would need to be an unprecedentedly rapid structural shift (like an AI apocalypse hitting only white-collar workers suddenly). With the forecast date in late April 2025, only three quarters remain in the target period (Q2, Q3, Q4 2025). The community prediction was at 7% as recently as mid-April 2025, implying it has not hit the threshold in any reported quarters to date (Q2 2023 through Q4 2024/Q1 2025). Moreover, Q1 2025 BLS data shows the "Management, professional, and related occupations" group actually grew by approximately 400,000 workers year-over-year. Without evidence of a massive, sudden collapse in white-collar employment, achieving a 2 percentage point drop in the remaining months is deeply implausible. I assign a low probability (5%) to this happening.
